title: Nashville Housing Data Cleaning
description: Cleaned and standardized 56,477 rows of Nashville housing data using SQL Server Management Studio. Removed duplicates, standardized date formats, split address fields, and populated null values — transforming raw municipal data into an analysis-ready dataset.
insights: Identified and removed hundreds of duplicate property records. Standardized 19 inconsistent column formats. Transformed raw address strings into structured city/state/zip fields. Result: a fully clean 56,477-row dataset with zero duplicates.
dataset_info: 56,477 rows, 19 columns. Source: Nashville municipal housing records. Covers property sales, owner addresses, land use classifications, and sale prices across Davidson County.
methodology: Used SQL Server Management Studio. Key operations: ISNULL/COALESCE for null handling, PARSENAME and SUBSTRING for address splitting, ROW_NUMBER() OVER PARTITION for duplicate detection, ALTER TABLE for column standardization.

title: BMI & Waist-to-Hip Ratio Calculator
description: Built an interactive health metrics calculator using Python (Anaconda/Jupyter). Computes BMI and waist-to-hip ratio with real-time category classification across 4 BMI ranges and sex-specific WHR thresholds.
insights: 4 BMI categories: Underweight (<18.5), Healthy Weight (18.5–24.9), Overweight (25–29.9), Above Obesity (≥30). WHR thresholds differ by biological sex. Tool provides instant clinical context for any height/weight combination.
dataset_info: Formula-based calculator — no external dataset. Uses WHO-standard BMI formula (weight kg / height m²) and sex-specific WHR risk thresholds from clinical guidelines.
methodology: Python with Jupyter Notebook. Libraries: pandas for data handling, ipywidgets for interactive inputs. BMI formula: weight(kg) / height(m)². WHR formula: waist(in) / hip(in).

title: Data Professional Survey Dashboard
description: Analyzed survey responses from 630 data professionals worldwide to uncover salary trends, tool preferences, and career satisfaction scores. Built an interactive Power BI dashboard with cross-filtering across demographics and roles.
insights: 630 participants, average age 29.87. Python is the most popular language among data professionals. Significant salary variation by job title — Data Scientists earn ~2x more than Data Analysts. Work/life balance rated higher than salary satisfaction on average.
dataset_info: 630 survey responses. Fields include: job title, salary, country, programming language, years of experience, education level, industry, and satisfaction scores (salary, work/life balance, coworkers, management, upward mobility, learning opportunities).
methodology: Data cleaned in Excel, visualized in Power BI. Used DAX measures for average calculations. Cross-filtering slicers by country, job title, and gender. KPI cards for headline metrics.

title: Airbnb Seattle 2016 Analysis
description: Analyzed 323,346 Airbnb listing entries across Seattle to identify revenue trends, pricing by location, and seasonal demand patterns. Built a multi-sheet Tableau dashboard with map, bar, and time-series views.
insights: Zip code 98134 commands the highest average listing price. Revenue peaks sharply on December 25th at $2.1M — holiday demand drives a clear seasonal spike. Listings with more bedrooms generate higher per-night revenue but lower occupancy rates.
dataset_info: 323,346 entries across 3 worksheets: Listings, Reviews, and Calendar. Covers all Seattle Airbnb activity in 2016. Fields include: zip code, price, number of bedrooms, availability, review scores, and host details.
methodology: Data joined across 3 sheets in Tableau using listing ID as the key. Built 5 views: avg price by zip (bar), yearly revenue (line), price by bedrooms (bar), homes by bedrooms (bar), and a full summary dashboard.

title: Bike Sales Dashboard
description: Built a dynamic sales performance dashboard in Microsoft Excel analyzing 1,026 bike purchase records. Used PivotTables, slicers, and custom charts to surface regional profit trends and customer demographic patterns.
insights: Pacific Region leads all regions in profit margins. Middle Age bracket (31–54) is the highest-converting customer segment. Commute distance of 0–1 miles correlates with the highest purchase rates. Male customers slightly outpace female in total purchases.
dataset_info: 1,026 rows, 13 columns. Fields: customer ID, marital status, gender, income, children, education, occupation, home ownership, cars, commute distance, region, age bracket, and purchase decision.
methodology: Microsoft Excel. Built PivotTables for regional and demographic segmentation. Used slicers for interactive filtering by marital status, region, and education. Custom bar and line charts for trend visualization.
